review_notes: The functions are applicable as they relate to managing service accounts in Kubernetes, which aligns with the FedRAMP AC-2 (11) control on account management and usage conditions. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but slight improvements could be made for clarity.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'k8s_rbac_service_account_token_expiry_configured' for brevity and consistency with common terminology.
title: Account Management | Usage Conditions
step1_reasoning: The FedRAMP AC-2 (11) control focuses on managing account usage conditions, such as ensuring that access keys or console access are not unused. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to managing service accounts, which are the equivalent of user accounts in cloud environments. The checks focus on ensuring that service accounts have defined usage conditions, such as token expiration and deletion of inactive accounts, to prevent unauthorized access.
